title: 10 possible side effects of Panax notoginseng (tienchi)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Panax notoginseng causes allergic reactions in some individuals due to its ability to stimulate the immune system. The saponin compounds present in Panax notoginseng can trigger an overactive response from the body's defenses, leading to symptoms such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, the herb's ability to increase white blood cell count may also contribute to allergic reactions by amplifying the body's natural defense mechanisms.

Panax notoginseng produces digestive upset in some individuals due to its ability to increase blood flow and stimulate digestion. The saponin content in the herb can cause irritation of the stomach lining, leading to symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and stomach discomfort. Additionally, the herb's stimulating effects on the gut can also trigger acid reflux and heartburn in sensitive individuals.

Panax notoginseng triggers headaches regularly due to its ability to stimulate the nervous system and increase blood flow to the brain. This stimulation can cause an overactive response, leading to headache symptoms such as throbbing pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and irritability. Additionally, Panax notoginseng's ability to dilate blood vessels can also contribute to increased blood pressure, further exacerbating headaches in some individuals.

Panax notoginseng increases blood pressure due to its ability to stimulate the sympathetic nervous system and increase levels of epinephrine in the body. This stimulates the heart to beat faster and more forcefully, leading to increased cardiac output and subsequent elevation in blood pressure. Additionally, Panax notoginseng can also cause vasoconstriction, which further contributes to the rise in blood pressure.

Panax notoginseng induces insomnia deeply due to its ability to increase the levels of stress hormones like cortisol and adrenaline in the body. This can lead to difficulty falling asleep and staying asleep, resulting in fatigue, irritability, and other related symptoms. Additionally, the herb's stimulatory effects on the nervous system may further exacerbate insomnia, making it challenging for individuals to get a good night's rest.

Panax notoginseng affects fertility negatively due to its ability to stimulate the production of sex hormones such as estrogen and testosterone. This can lead to an imbalance in hormone levels, potentially disrupting the normal functioning of the reproductive system and making it more challenging for individuals to conceive or maintain a healthy pregnancy. Additionally, Panax notoginseng's influence on the hypothalamic-pituitary axis may also contribute to its negative impact on fertility by affecting the body's natural hormonal rhythms.

Panax notoginseng raises serum creatinine levels in some individuals due to its ability to increase blood pressure and alter kidney function. The saponin content in Panax notoginseng can cause vasodilation, leading to increased renal blood flow and potentially elevated glomerular filtration rate, which may result in higher creatinine levels. This side effect is often seen in people with pre-existing kidney disease or hypertension, making it essential to monitor kidney function closely when using this herbal supplement.

Panax notoginseng enhances bleeding risk due to its ability to inhibit platelet aggregation and prolong bleeding time. This is caused by the saponin compounds present in the root, which can interact with blood-clotting factors and reduce their effectiveness. As a result, people taking Panax notoginseng may be more prone to bleeding or bruising, particularly if they are already taking anticoagulant medications.

Panax notoginseng reduces blood sugar levels by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in carbohydrate metabolism. This herb has been shown to decrease glucose uptake in skeletal muscles and increase glucose production in the liver, leading to a decrease in blood sugar levels. Additionally, Panax notoginseng may stimulate the release of insulin from pancreatic beta cells, further contributing to its hypoglycemic effects.

Panax notoginseng increases liver enzymes. This side effect is attributed to the plant's ability to stimulate the activity of certain enzymes in the liver, such as cytochrome P450, which are responsible for metabolizing drugs and toxins. As a result, Panax notoginseng may cause an increase in liver enzymes, which can indicate liver stress or inflammation.

intro: Panax notoginseng has some side effects when used improperly, such as digestive issues, insomnia, and allergic reactions. These side effects can occur due to the herb's high saponin content, which can irritate the stomach lining and disrupt sleep patterns. For example, if someone experiences persistent nausea and vomiting from using Panax notoginseng, it could lead to malnutrition, dehydration, and a weakened immune system, making them more prone to illnesses and affecting their daily routine.
intro_desc: Panax notoginseng has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, bleeding, and dizziness. These side effects may be caused by taking high doses or combining it with other medications that thin the blood. For instance, someone who already has a bleeding disorder may experience severe internal bleeding, leading to anemia, fatigue, and poor quality of life. Furthermore, dizziness can cause accidents and injuries, affecting daily activities and work performance.
